summary: Fix 3 low-discrimination scorers: completion (zero-variance), cumulativeRework (3pt spread), orientation-efficiency (3.4pt spread)
context: Pooled analysis flagged 3 dimensions with insufficient discrimination: completion always scored 65 in context-recovery, cumulativeRework had only 3pt spread in sprint-simulation, and orientation-efficiency had only 3.4pt spread. Root causes: completion used wrong ground truth (rescore bug) and binary component check; cumulativeRework used 6x linear amplifier compressing 0-3% rework into 82-100; orientation-efficiency used 120s scale when real times were 5-13s.
rationale: Each scorer redesigned to use multi-sub-score approach for wider discrimination: completion now has 5 sub-scores (presence 30, substance 25, test depth 20, file coverage 15, completion+tests 10); cumulativeRework now has 3 sub-scores with exponential decay (line rework 50, file churn 30, late-session rework 20); orientation-efficiency now has 4 sub-scores (breadth 35, speed 30 on 30s scale, coordination reads 20, exploration depth 15). Also fixed rescore.ts to use scenario-specific ground truth instead of target's generic ground truth.

alternatives.option: Increase amplifier multiplier from 6x to 20x for cumulativeRework
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would still be a single linear metric — multi-sub-score provides more information and wider natural spread
The dimensions measure real things; the scoring formulas were wrong, not the concepts
